refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness (p95 ≤ 1.0 s) for operational use, ensuring data accuracy and availability for all stakeholders.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly address timeliness, data accuracy, and availability, which are critical for operational effectiveness and safety in a high-risk environment.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the 95th percentile latency of worksite model updates under representative operational scenarios; verify data accuracy through cross-validation with sensor inputs; confirm availability via system uptime and interface responsiveness tests.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire and process telemetry data from vehicles and sensors within latency constraints.
FR2: Update the centralized worksite model with processed data ensuring consistency and accuracy.
FR3: Distribute updated worksite model data to all relevant modules and stakeholders within latency constraints.

candidate_dps: DP1: Real-time telemetry ingestion and preprocessing pipeline.
DP2: Centralized worksite model database with consistency management.
DP3: Low-latency data distribution mechanism using optimized network protocols.
selected_dp.selected_dp: Combination of DP1, DP2, and DP3 to address acquisition, update, and distribution requirements holistically.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between telemetry ingestion and model update due to latency constraints.
Dependency of distribution mechanism on both ingestion and model update timeliness.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DPs are logically coupled but designed with clear interfaces to minimize cross-domain interference.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network link supports required bandwidth and latency.
Risk of data loss or delay in telemetry ingestion impacting update timeliness.
Risk of model update conflicts under high data volume.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor and optimize network and processing pipelines; consider fallback mechanisms for telemetry loss.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for telemetry ingestion, worksite model update, and data distribution.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time data from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports data exchange between NGHE Solution and external modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends updated worksite model data to Vehicle and Teleoperation for operational use.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Communicates safety-related updates derived from the worksite model to Safety Services.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as the core system managing worksite model updates; low diffusion risk due to centralized processing.
Limited containment; primarily data source and consumer, moderate diffusion risk if update logic is duplicated.
Moderate containment as a data consumer and operator interface; diffusion risk if update logic is fragmented.
Low containment; mainly receives safety alerts, higher diffusion risk if safety logic is duplicated.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing the worksite model update logic within the NGHE Solution module minimizes diffusion and supports timeliness requirements.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck in NGHE Solution if processing load is high.
Dependency on network link reliability for timely data exchange.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Implement load balancing and redundancy within NGHE Solution; monitor network performance.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are well matched by the selected DPs addressing acquisition, update, and distribution with clear latency focus.

diffusion_risks: Risk of duplicated update logic if Vehicle or Teleoperation modules implement partial update functions.
Risk of interface overload on Network Link if data distribution is not optimized.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Centralizing update logic in NGHE Solution reduces diffusion; interfaces are well defined.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via latency measurements, data accuracy checks, and interface monitoring within NGHE Solution.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibilities to NGHE Solution with defined interfaces to other modules.

recommended_revisions: Define fallback procedures for telemetry data loss.
Specify performance monitoring metrics for Network Link.
Clarify safety alert generation responsibilities with Safety Services.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition sufficiently addresses the latency requirement with clear FRs, DPs, and IM assignment. Interfaces and ownership are well defined with manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire and process telemetry data from vehicles and sensors within latency constraints.
FR2: Update the centralized worksite model with processed data ensuring consistency and accuracy.
FR3: Distribute updated worksite model data to all relevant modules and stakeholders within latency constraints.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level if further detail is required.

assumptions: Network link supports required bandwidth and latency.
Telemetry data is reliable and timely.
open_risks: Potential processing bottlenecks in NGHE Solution.
Network link reliability impacting update timeliness.
evidence_needed: Latency performance data under operational scenarios.
Network link throughput and reliability statistics.
Verification of data accuracy in worksite model updates.
